issue_title: Collision in multi-process mode
issue_body:
Hello @brian-brazil.

In our project we use celery. And we experience a bug when all workers write to one file created by the master process. It's happening because metrics are initialized before fork and an open file is inherited by the child process. `_MmapedValue` in the child is created correctly, with new `pid`, so the child doesn't know that files belong to another process.

---
PR: https://github.com/prometheus/client_python/pull/192

issue_title: Numbers with finnish stemmer
issue_body:
When entering a number to finnish stemmer with 2 or more trailing zeroes, last zero is removed.

## Steps to reproduce
1. Open demo at snowballstem.org: http://snowballstem.org/demo.html
2. Select Finnish stemming algorithm
3. Enter a text including a nuber with two or more trailing zeroes, eg. "testattiin 2000"

## Expected result
1. Output will be "testat 2000"

## Actual result
1. Output is "testat 200" (last zero is gone)

issue_title: Use Wheels on PyPI
issue_body: Right now, `pip install imageio` downloads the Linux tarball and associated `freeimage` library.  Wheels are preferred downloads for pip.

issue_title: Add `shell=True` to `suprocess.Popen` calls (Pyinstaller)
issue_body:
I can confirm that imageio+ffmpeg works with Pyinstaller on Windows and Anaconda Python 2.7 (#32).
However, when using the [ffmpeg](https://github.com/imageio/imageio/blob/master/imageio/plugins/ffmpeg.py) plugin in a frozen application on Windows, and when seeking through an avi video (`imageio.get_reader`) using `get_data`, an empty command Window pops up with the title ("path/to/ffmpeg-win32-v3.2.4.exe"). These command windows are not shown when the keyword argument `shell=True` is set in `sp.Popen`: https://github.com/paulmueller/imageio/commit/b5aeb617fb6b1a74ff30c40ae998bac31b526f39

If this does not break compatibility, I would recommend adding that extra keyword argument everywhere. I can create a pull request if you are too busy yourself.

issue_title: Not working jpmml-converter version with GradientBoostingClassifier
issue_body:
The jpmml-converter-1.2.3.jar included in the current release, generates a wrong PMML document.

The second segment of the multiple model does not read the output produced by the first one (tree ensemble), generating a constant prediction.

This is an example of segment generated with the version 1.2.3
```
<Segment id="2">
    <True/>
    <RegressionModel functionName="classification" normalizationMethod="none">
        <MiningSchema>
            <MiningField name="TARGET" usageType="target"/>
        </MiningSchema>
        <Output>
            <OutputField name="probability(-1)" optype="continuous" dataType="double" feature="probability" value="-1"/>
            <OutputField name="probability(1)" optype="continuous" dataType="double" feature="probability" value="1"/>
        </Output>
        <RegressionTable intercept="1.0" targetCategory="1"/>
        <RegressionTable intercept="0.0" targetCategory="-1"/>
    </RegressionModel>
</Segment>
```

This is the same segment generated with a version >= 1.2.4
```
<Segment id="2">
    <True/>
    <RegressionModel functionName="classification" normalizationMethod="none">
        <MiningSchema>
            <MiningField name="TARGET" usageType="target"/>
            <MiningField name="transformedDecisionFunction(1)"/>
        </MiningSchema>
        <Output>
            <OutputField name="probability(-1)" optype="continuous" dataType="double" feature="probability" value="-1"/>
            <OutputField name="probability(1)" optype="continuous" dataType="double" feature="probability" value="1"/>

issue_title: consider making learner sign-in feature a 'fuzzy' search
issue_body:
Currently, the auto-complete of learner usernames only begins when three characters are entered, and only matches usernames that exactly start with those characters. Since the purpose is to help learners get in easily, and typos/forgotten usernames will be common, recommend implementing this as a "fuzzy" search where matches can occur anywhere in the string, and don't need to be contiguous.

This would require a bit more optimization in the way we perform lookups, and also some smart sorting and filtering so the user doesn't see a list of hundreds of matches.

issue_title: Versioning
issue_body:
There are two possible ways to deal with versioning:
1. Have different packages for different api version.
2. Allow wrappers to adapt to new versions.

I like option 1 because it keeps resource mapping simpler, but in other hand seems much more work having to create a new wrapper for minor changes. Eg.: facebook's API has 2.3 and 2.4 versions with very few changes.
We should look into how Raml and Swagger deal with it. If they natively support versioning, I'm in favor of also supporting it.

issue_title: 1 handler slow can lead to delayed collection of metrics
issue_body:
Hi, 

Our diamond agents are configured with on average 15 collectors. Depending on the environment of the server, they collect and publish every 60 seconds or 5 minutes. We use 2 handlers: `GraphiteHandler` and `ArchiveHandler`. The `ArchiveHandler` keeps its data for only few days and is used as a backup in case something is wrong with our graphite server, so we can resubmit the missing datapoints.

Last weekend we had an issue with our graphite setup, causing the `GraphiteHandler` to have timeouts and reconnects. Since the call to the process method of the `GraphiteHandler` is synchronized among all collectors, all collectors took 225sec (15 * 15sec) to "publish" to graphite. As such new datapoints were not collected at every run interval.

Besides making our graphite setup more robust, are there currently options to influence this behavior?

As fix I was thinking on adding an option to the `GraphiteHandler` that would specify how much time must have passed before reconnecting after a failed connection attempt. There could even be 2 behaviors: 
- The handler reconnects after the configured/fixed time 
- The retry time is doubled after every attempt until a configured maximum is reached. After reaching the maximum, it would no longer increase the retry interval. After a successfull connection, the retry interval would be reset obviously.

Discuss: Create deis etcd folders as first action
issue_body:
Today every deis unit has an etcd folder, which is created in its app/boot script (e.g. https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/master/cache/bin/boot#L29) The problem is, that confd doesn't allow to subscribe to a none existing folder and error-prone workarounds are required to ensure that the folder exists (https://github.com/johanneswuerbach/deis/commit/99a2c1438ff9a6225b6aa57d6a1e4af3331f30c1), even when the actual values are optional (e.g. the registry can use deis cache, but doesn't requires it).

I think it would be easier to create all `deis/component` folders before starting deis units to allow confd subscription and simplify the startup process.

issue_title: Define a new option type which strictly requires that it is only passed once
issue_body:
We're currently using the Click default behavior when parsing options with `multiple=False`.
That parses all instances of an option and returns the last one given.

So `globus transfer ls --endpoint-id 'abc123' --endpoint-id 'def456'` is entirely valid, and results in `endpoint_id="def456"`.

This is fine/safe for options where allowing an override doesn't change the service-side semantics of commands, like `--format`, but for IDs and paths, it could be the result of user confusion and not result in the desired action.
For example, a `globus transfer async-transfer` with two sources, two destinations, and two paths is inherently ambiguous, and likely means that the user has wholly misunderstood usage of that command.

To correctly implement a change in this behavior, I think we need to add a new class of option.
Such opts can internally be specified with `multiple=True` and then assert that `len(value) <= 1` where `value` is the resulting tuple.
I'd suggest that this be a custom callback, but I worry about future opts that need or want a custom callback _and_ this behavior.
The custom option class doesn't add much more complexity, and properly handles that case.

issue_title: Javascript Support
Enable logging to file by default for alpha releases
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Users of the alpha releases are very likely going to encounter errors for which we need debug level logging output to diagnose.  They however are unlikely to be both logging at the `DEBUG` level **and** retaining log output.

### How can it be fixed

Lets enable file based logging in a `$XDG_TRINITY_ROOT/<chain-dir>/logs` at the `DEBUG` level.  This way when a user has an issue we can get them to dig out the relevant logfiles for us.

norm_address[i] should not invoke `upper()`. 

In that case, address is '0x8eb009288b68c343de5bc393a7e13c28f5425780', checksum_address is '0x8EB009288b68C343DE5Bc393a7e13C28F5425780'. 

`is_checksum_address(value)` returns `False`

issue_title: Make virtualenv an extra requirement
issue_body:
Virtualenv is currently a requirement because it is used in wagon validation but is not mandatory for creating and installing wagons.

Should we provide it as an extra requirement so that users can install it via `pip install wagon['validate']` instead?

issue_title: "root" environment needs a different name
issue_body:
`/root` is what the lay user will think the "*root environment*" refers to.  *root* is fairly universally assumed to mean *things associated with the root super user*.

I very strongly believe, even though it is late in the day, we need to rename *root environment* to something like *base environment* that will not create this confusion.

issue_title: Confused about upgrade vs downgrade?
issue_body:
Conda sees this as an upgrade:

```
    r-zoo:          1.7_12-r3.2.2_1a    r --> 1.7_13-r3.3.1_0    r
```

but then sees this as a downgrade:

```
    r-assertthat:   0.1-r3.2.2_2a       r --> 0.1-r3.3.1_2       r
```

That seems surprising.  This is with Conda 4.1.11.

issue_title: Allow condarc to specify a trusted cert path for ssl
issue_body:
Currently the `.condarc` file's `verify_ssl` key seems to be just a boolean (see https://github.com/conda/conda/blob/master/conda/config.py#L70).  If a proxy is in the way, the only option [1] is to turn off ssl verification.  However, requests supports setting the verify_ssl setting to a file of trusted certificates (see http://docs.python-requests.org/en/latest/user/advanced/#ssl-cert-verification).  Can we allow the .condarc verify_ssl setting to take a path to a certificate file?  Then we don't have to forego the ssl verification, but we can still trust the proxy's man-in-the-middle certificate.

[1] Well, technically we can also set the REQUESTS_CA_CERTIFICATE environment variable to have requests trust a specific list of certificates, but that requires (a) digging into the conda source to see that it is using requests, and then (b) digging into the requests docs to see that you can set the environment variable.  It'd be much cleaner if the .condarc setting just supported the path too.

issue_title: Package pyside issues
issue_body:
My application is based on numpy+traitsui and requires pyside, enable, chaco.

For library pyside, versions 1.1.1 and 1.1.2 are available.

* Version 1.1.1 dates back to 2012-09-13. 
* Only requirements are python 2.7
* Does not work because it contains library version 1.1.1.1 but it tries to load 1.1.1 which should be there but is missing.

    import PySide.QtCore  # @UnresolvedImport
    ImportError: dlopen(/Applications/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/PySide/QtCore.so, 2): 
    Library not loaded: @loader_path/../../../libpyside-python2.7.1.1.dylib
    Referenced from: /Applications/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/PySide/QtCore.so
    Reason: image not found

* Version 1.1.2 dates back to 2013-05-04
* Dependencies include qt 4.7.4 _exactly_ which is too low for pyqt which is required by enable which is required by chaco.

I see no way to install pyside+enable+chaco. With 1.1.1 it would mesh (albeit with oldish packages), though it does not work due to the missing 1.1.1 library. With 1.1.2 the 4.7.4 requirement would need to be relaxed to better than 4.7.4.

I have made it work by duplicating the library file and editing the name to remove the extra '.1',  but to distribute the application it needs to work with the standard packages.

What can I do?

Hi Team Conda,

Great work on conda! I have a small issue to report: when you run out of disk space and try to install a package with conda it will tell you there is a permissions error. 

Might it be possible to distinguish incorrect permissions from insufficient diskspace? I'm not sure if it would be of general utility for a broader audience but I would gather this is going to happen time-to-time, especially if using conda on a machine with shared resources/quotas.

Thanks,
zach cp

issue_title: ghdl architecture obsolated
issue_body:
Hello I'm using following script to run vunit tb:
```
from vunit import VUnit
from subprocess import call

# Make sure hdl lib is up to date
call(["make", "VHDL_STD=08"], cwd="../../")

# Create VUnit instance by parsing command line arguments
vu = VUnit.from_argv()

vu.add_external_library("hdl_lib", "../../")

# Create library 'lib'
lib = vu.add_library("lib")

# Add all files ending in .vhd in current working directory to library
lib.add_source_file("axis_fifo_tb.vhd")

vu.main()
```

Entity axis_fifo.vhd is part of hdl_lib library, while test bench axis_fifo_tb.vhd is not. When I call python run.py for the first time it works. When I modify axis_fifo.vhd it is recompiled in library, however I I got following error from ghdl:
```
architecture "bench" of "axis_fifo_tb" is obsoleted by entity "axis_fifo"
```

Then I need to use --clean to force vunit to recompile axis_fifo_tb.vhd. In fact it can be ghdl issue - it should not be needed to reanalyse axis_fifo_tb.vhd as it was not changed...

issue_title: Tab completion for VM extension names is incorrect
issue_body:
Actual output:
```
$ az vm extension show -g derek1 --vm-name dvmencrypt -n <TAB>
dvmencrypt/AzureDiskEncryptionForLinux               dvmencrypt/VMBackupForLinuxExtension                 swarm-master-80E5A796-0/linuxdiagnostic
dvmencrypt/Microsoft.Insights.VMDiagnosticsSettings  swarm-master-80E5A796-0/configuremaster    
```

Expected output:
```
$ az vm extension show -g derek1 --vm-name dvmencrypt -n <TAB>
AzureDiskEncryptionForLinux               VMBackupForLinuxExtension                 linuxdiagnostic
Microsoft.Insights.VMDiagnosticsSettings  configuremaster    
```

The completer for the `vm_extension_name` parameter of `vm extension` command is defined as `completer=get_resource_name_completion_list('Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/extensions')` which looks correct.

However, ARM returns vm extension names prefixed by the vm name (see below) which causes tab completion to show results in the format <vm_name>/<extension_name>.

```
$ az resource list -g derek1 --query "[?type=='Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/extensions'].{name:name, type:type}"
[
  {
    "name": "dvmencrypt/AzureDiskEncryptionForLinux",
    "type": "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/extensions"
  },
  {
    "name": "dvmencrypt/Microsoft.Insights.VMDiagnosticsSettings",
    "type": "Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/extensions"
  },
  ...
]
```

---

Possible solution, don't use the generic `get_resource_name_completion_list` method for this completer and instead create a method that removes the vm name prefix.

issue_title: BotService Command Review Comments
issue_body:
See here for documentation on extensions:
https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/tree/master/doc/extensions

Since this issue is already open, feel free to reference it in your PR there.

**Blocking**
- [x] `bot create`: kind needs enum value (https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/blob/dev/doc/authoring_command_modules/authoring_commands.md#registering-enums)

issue_title: [Question] Recommended way to get a raw string value from a custom query
issue_body: Since the JSON output type will return quoted value strings (e.g. `"foo"` instead of `foo`), what is the recommended solution for getting the raw/unquoted value of a string that is returned from using a custom `--query` flag? Using `-otsv` seems to work, but feels kind of "hacky" (?), and obviously piping JSON output to `jq -r` would work, but seems strange to need to use JQ strictly to remove the quotes from a string. Maybe `-otsv` is the right solution, and I'm just hung up on the name. Is the `tsv` output expected to be the "raw" format for scalar values? Is there a way to achieve this behavior via JMESPath? I looked at the spec but couldn't find anything. Either way, I'd love to hear what the recommended solution is for this, since I'm probably missing something.

issue_title: Storage queue service should automatically encode/decode base 64
issue_body:
### Description:
The other SDKs (.NET, Node.js) do that, so if you want to interop with them, it's currently the user's responsibility to do the encoding/decoding when using the Python SDK.

We should make it transparent and automatically encode/decode base64 when sending/receiving queue messages.

This would be a breaking change.

If we don't do this, we should fix the docs at http://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/documentation/articles/storage-python-how-to-use-queue-storage to show the encoding/decoding code required on the client's side.

##### Original Issue: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/issues/150
### Comments (2):
#### @cheif commented on 2014-04-25 15:01:51:
> I totally agree, the main idea of a SDK is to abstract away the implementation-details of the underlying resource, such as the transport using base64.

I think the best way to break the API is to bundle the breaking changes into a new major release (that would be 1.0.0 in this case), I think that there are some other things that also could be have a better abstraction (e.g. that StorageQueue.get_messages() should be a generator an not take a num_messages argument, that is also a leaky abstraction) or could be more pythonic (e.g. that QueueMessage-objects should be able to delete themselves, without using a StorageQueue-object, and a Blob-object being able to read their own data)

EDIT:
I realize that the "more-pythonic" changes can be implemented without breaking the API, I'd be happy to create a PR if others also think it's a good idea?

#### @saschagottfried commented on 2014-05-28 09:22:53:
> I had this issue this week with version 0.8.0 while developing code that receives queue messages transporting just a GUID that was inserted by .NET code. Since the .NET client did not insert XML or JSON into queue message I did not recognize the data as a base64 string. After checking .NET code is using usual code to insert queues messages, I eventually found MSDN information regarding base64 encoding/decoding of queue message. 

I expected this topic to be in discussion, so I am happy that package developers themselves make that an issue.

base64 decoding was not a big deal. The real problem at least for me was that I, as a developer, was not aware of that need to decode. Help developer to be aware that there are conditions where message.message_text is a base64 encoded string and therefore has to be decoded. And yes, update your docs as a first step towards a more pythonic Message Queue API. Otherwise you may risk to reduce Python developer satisfaction. That's not what you want since Azure rocks.

@cheif 
just do it and make a pull request. Open source is meant for contributions.

issue_title: ProcessPoolExecutor in background cause freeze after keep-alive timeout
issue_body:
## Long story short

If we run heavy task in `ProcessPoolExecutor` in background, `aiohttp` don't handle request after some timeout. It happens after ~75 seconds. It's equal `keepalive_timeout` value at https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/2.0.7/aiohttp/web_protocol.py#L82
If we change `keepalive_timeout` value we get freeze after specific time.

## Expected behaviour

Handle all requests.

## Actual behaviour

Freeze on request after keep-alive timeout.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Install deps
```console
pip install aiohttp requests
```

2. Run server
```python
import asyncio
import logging
from multiprocessing import Manager
from concurrent.futures import ProcessPoolExecutor

from aiohttp.web import Application, Response, run_app


logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)


async def index(request):
    body = '''<!DOCTYPE html>
    <html>
    <body>
    <script>
        function request(path) {
            var xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();
            
            xhr.open('GET', path, false);
            xhr.onload = function (e) {
                if (xhr.readyState == 4 && xhr.status == 200) {
                    console.log(xhr.responseText);
                }
            }
            xhr.send(null);
        }
        
        function start() {
            return request('/start');
        }
        
        function stop() {
            return request('/stop');
        }
    </script>
    <button style="width: 200px; height: 100px;" onclick="start()"> Start </button>
    <button style="width: 200px; height: 100px;" onclick="stop()"> Stop </button>
    </body>
    </html>
    '''
    return Response(body=body, content_type='document')


async def start(request):
    await request.app['worker'].queue.put('data')
    return Response(body='Start')


async def stop(request):
    request.app['worker'].stop_event.set()
    return Response(body='Stop')


def hard_work(stop_event):
    while True:
        if stop_event.is_set():
            break


class Worker:

    async def start(self, loop):
        self.queue = asyncio.Queue(loop=loop)

        while True:
            data = await self.queue.get()

            executor = ProcessPoolExecutor()
            manager = Manager()
            stop_event = manager.Event()
            self.stop_event = stop_event

            hard_future = loop.run_in_executor(executor, hard_work, stop_event)

            try:
                await asyncio.wait_for(hard_future, timeout=999999, loop=loop)
            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
                print('Time out')
                stop_event.set()
            else:
                print('Done')


def get_app():
    app = Application()

    app.router.add_get('/', index)
    app.router.add_get('/start', start)
    app.router.add_get('/stop', stop)


    async def start_worker(app):
        app.loop.set_debug(True)

        worker = Worker()
        app['worker'] = worker
        app.loop.create_task(worker.start(app.loop))

    app.on_startup.append(start_worker)

    return app


def main():
    app = get_app()
    run_app(app, host='0.0.0.0', port=5000)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

3. Make first `/start` request. Wait >75 seconds. Make second `/stop` request. `aiohttp` will not handle second `/stop` request.
```python
import time
import requests


def main():
    session = requests.Session()

    print('GET /start')
    response = session.get('http://127.0.0.1:5000/start')
    print('Response:', response.text)

    wait = 75 + 5
    print('Sleep in {} seconds'.format(wait))
    time.sleep(wait)

    print('GET /stop')
    response = session.get('http://127.0.0.1:5000/stop')
    print('Response:', response.text)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

## Your environment

Debian 8 Jessie
Python 3.5.3
aiohttp == 2.0.7

issue_title: Client Session Timeouts - Usage Question
issue_body:
## Long story short

We are using client sessions for the lifetime of the application as described here. 

> Unless you are connecting to a large, unknown number of different servers over the lifetime of your application, it is suggested you use a single session for the lifetime of your application to benefit from connection pooling

However. sometimes after running for several days, no requests through the session work. Every request results in a Timeout and Cancelled error shown below. The services which aiohttp are attempting to call are heathy and responding in less than 200ms when this occurs. The only resolution appears to be to kill and restart the process where the aiohttp client is running:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py", line 529, in __aenter__
    self._resp = yield from self._coro
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py", line 165, in _request
    conn = yield from self._connector.connect(req)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/async_timeout/__init__.py", line 44, in __exit__
    raise asyncio.TimeoutError from None

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiohttp/client.py", line 165, in _request
    conn = yield from self._connector.connect(req)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py", line 306, in connect
    yield from self._create_connection(req)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py", line 585, in _create_connection
    transport, proto = yield from self._create_direct_connection(req)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/aiohttp/connector.py", line 608, in _create_direct_connection
    local_addr=self._local_addr)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/asyncio/base_events.py", line 762, in create_connection
    yield from self.sock_connect(sock, address)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/asyncio/selector_events.py", line 451, in sock_connect
    return (yield from fut)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/asyncio/futures.py", line 380, in __iter__
    yield self  # This tells Task to wait for completion.
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/asyncio/tasks.py", line 304, in _wakeup
    future.result()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/asyncio/futures.py", line 285, in result
    raise CancelledError
concurrent.futures._base.CancelledError
```

## Expected behaviour

The session correctly creates and closes behavior through the lifetime of the application as documentation describes above. I have read in past github issues it seems people have trouble if trying to manually re-instantiate later in the life time of the application as well. If this is not the correct usage, then I would like to understand that and documentation to reflect how to handle when the session is no longer "good".

## Actual behaviour

After running for a period of days, the session becomes unresponsive and no future requests work without a timeout.

## Steps to reproduce

I haven't reproduced this manually yet. It happens in our deployed environments about every week. We migrated from requests where we did not see these stability issues.

## Your environment

Python 3.5. Docker official container (Debian). aiohttp==1.1.6 (I am open to an upgrade but we had planned to field soon so I want to understand correct usage and want to be certain an upgrade would fix. I'd rather be certain the code pattern could handle failure cases).

issue_title: Some session URLs are urlencoded twice
issue_body: As part of my testing of JupyterHub in our corporate network, users are logged in and a URL is populated with the full username as authenticated by PAM. In setups like ours, usernames are prefixed with a domain, such as DOMAIN\username, which then is built into the Notebook URL. Almost all of the calls only call URL `utils.url_join_encode` on the URL once, resulting in a valid url like `/user/DOMAIN%5Cusername/api/kernels/b6c1f2f0-c864-4933-b360-5bb61e922efc/channels`, however there are two instances that call it twice, resulting in double-escaped URLs like  `/user/DOMAIN%255Cusername/api/kernels/b6c1f2f0-c864-4933-b360-5bb61e922efc/channels`

issue_title: Node De-Duplication
issue_body:
Npm v3+ installs are maximally flat by default, but versions before that require `npm dedupe` to ensure that two versions of the same package with the same version are not installed.  This is a problem when a module stores module-private data structures that are meant to be shared and loaded only once.

I propose that the version of npm be checked on install, and if it is 3+, install with no modification.  Otherwise, attempt to run `npm dedupe` after `npm install`.  If that fails (as has been seen due to file permissions [issues](https://github.com/jupyter/atom-notebook/issues/32#issuecomment-184922403)), prompt the user to install npm 3+.  

I can make the necessary changes to `setupbase.py`.

issue_title: Stop modifying override dirs and split up LocalModule
issue_body:
With the abolition of the build field, there's no longer any need to modify overridde dirs. In particular, we no longer need to apply imports to them on disk. Instead (when we have recursive peru working and remote modules can have imports again), imports should be merged after the module is read into cache.

The `LocalModule` should be split up. We shouldn't use the same abstraction for override dirs as we use for the project directory anymore. This is a legacy from when we envisioned running build commands inside of override dirs in a transparent way, just like we were running build commands inside the project. Instead, we should think of an "override module" as a read-only source for a file tree, and nothing more.

issue_title: Model state format
issue_body:
At the moment, the state of the model at any given time is captured by the model object itself, and by the agent objects under it. These are all dynamic, and change with every activation. The data collector stores a subset of the model state values, and the server generates a static data structure that's tied to the specific visualization.

An idea for an alternative approach is defining a static model state data structure. This could be as simple as a JSON-type object which stores (by default) all model- and agent-level variable values at the time its called. This could supplement the current DataCollector, and potentially provide a standardized data format to send the client side of the visualization. It would also make it easier to log and store an entire run for later analysis, or for scrubbing forward and back video-style. The downside is that JSON specifically is heavy with lots of redundancies (especially if we store each agent's unique_id with hundreds+ of agents and hundreds+ of steps). That might make sending data to the browser slower. Even a more efficient format might slow models down by collecting and storing a lot of redundant or unnecessary data.

issue_title: TZID is not supported in rrule DTSTART
issue_body:
```python
from dateutil import rrule
rrule.rrulestr('DTSTART;TZID=America/New_York:20180119T130000Z RRULE:FREQ=DAILY;INTERVAL=1;COUNT=7')

File "~/venvs/tmp-c74b801dfc74a8d7/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dateutil/rrule.py", line 1616, in __call__

  raise ValueError("unsupported DTSTART parm: "+parm)
ValueError: unsupported DTSTART parm: TZID=AMERICA/NEW_YORK
```

From https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5545.txt:

>       The following are examples of this property parameter:
> 
>        DTSTART;TZID=America/New_York:19980119T020000
> 
>        DTEND;TZID=America/New_York:19980119T030000
> 
>       The "TZID" property parameter MUST NOT be applied to DATE
>       properties and DATE-TIME or TIME properties whose time values are
>       specified in UTC.

Is there a way to accomplish this in `dateutil` that I'm unaware of?

issue_title: checkFormat raises exception for workflow input set by default to Null
issue_body:
## Expected Behavior
When optional workflow input `input_file` with a `format` field is not provided and the `default: null` is used the `checkFormat` function shouldn't raise exception when trying to check `format` for this input.

## Actual Behavior
`checkFormat` function raises exception for optional workflow input with `default: null` and `format` fields.
Workflow doesn't fail if:
- remove either `default: null` or `format`
- remove `namespaces` and `schemas` sections

## Workflow Code
```yaml
cwlVersion: v1.0
class: Workflow

inputs:
  input_file:
    type: File?
    default: null
    format: "http://edamontology.org/format_2572"

outputs: []

steps:
  cat:
    run: cat.cwl
    in:
      input_file: input_file
    out: []

$namespaces:
  s: http://schema.org/
$schemas:
- http://schema.org/docs/schema_org_rdfa.html
s:name: "workflow"
```
```yaml
cwlVersion: v1.0
class: CommandLineTool

hints:
  DockerRequirement:
    dockerPull: ubuntu

inputs:
  input_file:
    type:
      - "null"
      - File

issue_title: Coveralls doesn't link files correctly [minor]
issue_body:
The reason is that our `.travis.yml` recipe is designed to test the *installed* package rather than just the local checkout. On the one hand, this is nice, because detects problems due to modules not being included in the installation. On the other hand, it obviously confuses coveralls which cannot match the absolute path name of the installed file in the virtualenv to the file in the github repository.

Solutions:
- ignore?
- change directory before executing tests?
- just test the local checkout?

issue_title: using b2 command line tool as a library
issue_body:
I would like to implement something in python using b2 and its command line tool. Current implementation already lets me import it (after I symlink it to `b2.py`, that is) and call functions on it, which is good, however for any application which is not console-based or requires some resiliency against errors, it is impossible to use due to the current architecture. It is also not possible to test it automatically in isolation. Clearly the current codebase is already designed with integration with other python code - at least partially.

The problem is with two layers being mixed with each other. One layer is what performs operations on the backend (let's call it controller) and the other is what displays the retrieved information (let's call it view). For example, if I would like to create a GUI application which would present the result of `ls`, I can't, because the function uses `print` to return results to the user. Here the layers should be divided so that the backend function returns an iterator of entries (objects), which would then be printed by the view function. If someone would need to call a library equivalent of `ls`, he would call the backend one and then display the result in the GUI (or web page or whatever he is trying to build).

Another example is that if something does not exist or there is some other problem, current tool calls `sys.exit()`. If the user supplies invalid data to the gui application, as an application developer I want to display an error message, not shutdown the whole program. Therefore I'd expect the library functions to raise an exception on error, so that it can be handled properly.

It is possible to implement most of the functionality of b2 command line tool again in a way which has properly separated layers and allows for console, gui, web page and other types of outputs, as well as for automated testing in isolation.

On the other hand, the same changes could be developed here in this repository, with keeping most of the interface as it is. To be precise, I'd contribute most (if not all) of the code and tests required to do this properly. As it is a larger amount of work, I'd like to discuss it first here, so if my view and views of official maintainers are not aligned, it can be compared before much work is put into development.

Please comment the above.

issue_title: Allow several schemas, equivalent from client POV, to resolve to the same definition
issue_body:
It is possible in an application to have multiple serialization/deserialization schemas that look identical to the client but behave differently in the application.

This is the case in a MongoDB application of mine where I use an Object Document Mapper. My app code may return an object, in which case it gets marshalled using a "OO <-> json" schema. But sometimes I have to sort of skip the ODM (because I'm using the aggregation framework) and I have a "MongoDB <-> json" schema.

You could think of other examples, like multi-DB and an object that can be stored in both.

The point is all the schema attributes are the same (same fields are dump_only, etc.) but they may deserialize with different field names, for instance, or one schema may instantiate an object and the other just return plain json.

This is internal stuff, it should not be exposed to the client. So I'd like to be able to register both schemas with the same definition in the docs.

One would be the master, the one used by `schema2jsonschema` to provide a definition in the docs. The other one(s) would just refer to that definition.

I realize this might not be a common use case, but I don't think it would add too much complexity.

It could be 

1/ Implicit

```py
spec.definition('User', schema=UserObjectSchema)
spec.definition('User', schema=UserDBSchema)
```

On the second call, [schema_definition_helper](https://github.com/marshmallow-code/apispec/blob/14be6d8be8608dec4af9e215a154e521f9d8ff78/apispec/ext/marshmallow/__init__.py#L37) knows a Schema is already declared and considers the first as the master, the second as the clone.

2/ Explicit

```py
spec.definition('User', schema=UserObjectSchema)
spec.definition('User', schema=UserDBSchema, clone=True)
```

but I don't really see the need for that explicit version.

This would impact 

- [schema_definition_helper](https://github.com/marshmallow-code/apispec/blob/14be6d8be8608dec4af9e215a154e521f9d8ff78/apispec/ext/marshmallow/__init__.py#L37): check whether a shema with the same name is already registered
- [APISpec.definition](https://github.com/marshmallow-code/apispec/blob/14be6d8be8608dec4af9e215a154e521f9d8ff78/apispec/core.py#L195): add new exception allowing a definition_helper to stop the registration process so that no definition is actually added.

Any comment before I submit a PR?

issue_title: About bpr loss implementation and regularization
issue_body:
Hi @maciejkula , 
LightFM helps me a lot in my learning recommendation algorithms, thank you. I am very interested in the implementation of bpr and warp losses in lightfm.
I read the BPR paper [https://arxiv.org/pdf/1205.2618.pdf ](url), in which they maximize BPR-OPT.
![github1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34033376/40474802-8ec657f6-5f72-11e8-85fa-313c0af9a0ec.PNG)
Use SGD to minimize the negative of BPR, the gradient is as following fig, equation 1.
![github4](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34033376/40477891-315cd164-5f7a-11e8-97a7-55987d84f296.PNG)


I studied the method warp_update in _lightfm_fast.pyx.template, and found that the gradients were calculated in the following way.
loss = 1 - sigmoid(x_uij)
1. gradient for biases of positive item: -loss
2. for biases of negative item: loss
3. for biases of user: loss
4. for embeddings of positive item: -loss * p_u
5. for embeddings of negative item: loss * p_u
6. for embedding of user: -loss * (q_i - q_j)

![github3](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34033376/40478101-c40abad0-5f7a-11e8-8172-4578313bb1d7.PNG)
So there are two points I don't understand:
The calculations of gradients 1, 2, 4, 5, 6 seem right to me, but isn't biases of user (b_u) subtracted by itself, as the above fig? Why gradient for biases of user equals loss.
Gradients seem to be caculated by the first part of equation 1, so it is ok to leave out the second part of equation 1? 
Maybe I missed something important, I would appreciate it very much if you could give me some help.



![github5](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/34033376/40484436-3a538b78-5f8e-11e8-881e-a1ab0dbd3301.PNG)
And about regularization, I searched "lazy regularization" and "weight decay" in google, but couldn't find useful explaination to help me understand why `features[feature, component] *= (1.0 + alpha * local_learning_rate)` works. Are there any blog post explaining this theory, or could you please tell me keywords which I can use to look up. Thank you very much.

issue_title: Parametrized factory (reusable factory)
issue_body:
I have different Service Objects which have a Database object injected in order to abstract from the ORM (better testability). 
The default implementation is an SqlAlchemy implementation, which has 2 parameters, a base_class and a session.

I have these two factories:

    tokens = providers.Factory(services.TokenService,
                               id_generator=id_generator,
                               database=providers.Factory(
                                   services.SqlAlchemyDatabaseService,
                                   session=session,
                                   base_class=models.Token)
                               )

    users = providers.Factory(services.UserService,
                               id_generator=id_generator,
                               database=providers.Factory(
                                   services.SqlAlchemyDatabaseService,
                                   session=session,
                                   base_class=models.User)
                               )

As you can see, the ```database``` factory should be moved away, so the parameters that are the same for all usages are in only one place. Ideally, I would like to have something like:

    database = providers.Factory(services.SqlAlchemyDatabaseService,
                                 session=session)

    tokens = providers.Factory(services.TokenService,
                               id_generator=id_generator,
                               database=database.extend(base_class=models.Token)

So database lacks one parameter, which could be provided by some decorator like that hypothetical ```extend()``` method. Is it something that is already implemented?

taupage-init: INFO: Waiting for berry to download OAuth credentials to /meta/credentials/user.json..
berry: ERROR: Could not read from mint S3 bucket "zalando-stups-mint-607300429032-eu-central-1": 400 Bad Request: The authorization mechanism you have provided is not supported. Please use AWS4-HMAC-SHA256.

> AWS4-HMAC-SHA256, also known as "V4," is one of two authentication schemes in S3.
>
US-Standard, and many -- but not all -- other regions, also support the other, older scheme, V2
Since Frankfurt was introduced late in 2014, it apparently does not support V2, which is what this error suggests you are using.

http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/UsingAWSSDK.html explains how to enable V4 in the various SDKs, assuming you are using an SDK that has that capability.

issue_body: As reported in https://github.com/dmlc/gluon-nlp/pull/136, some functions can be greatly accelerated using C implementations. Would it be worthwhile to add a C library to hold these functions? In fact, FastText are generally implemented in C++  (https://github.com/facebookresearch/fastText/tree/master/src) and the bleu score in fairseq is also implemented in C (https://github.com/pytorch/fairseq/tree/master/fairseq/clib/libbleu).

issue_title: Tutorial subtype example appears wrong
issue_body:
I think I’ve found an issue with the z3 tutorial page at http://rise4fun.com/Z3Py/tutorial/guide . In the subtype example (under Modeling with Quantifiers), I think the assertion about root-type has the argument order reversed, so it is declaring root-type to be a subtype of all other types instead of the reverse.  Given the single inheritance assertion, that would imply that the type hierarchy must be a single linear order, not a tree.  The following transcript based on the example shows the problem.  

```
$ z3 -in
(declare-sort Type)
(declare-fun subtype (Type Type) Bool)
(declare-fun array-of (Type) Type)
(assert (forall ((x Type)) (subtype x x)))
(assert (forall ((x Type) (y Type) (z Type))
          (=> (and (subtype x y) (subtype y z))
              (subtype x z))))
(assert (forall ((x Type) (y Type))
          (=> (and (subtype x y) (subtype y x))
              (= x y))))
(assert (forall ((x Type) (y Type) (z Type))
          (=> (and (subtype x y) (subtype x z))
              (or (subtype y z) (subtype z y)))))
(assert (forall ((x Type) (y Type))
          (=> (subtype x y)
              (subtype (array-of x) (array-of y)))))
; Check that subtypes are only a partial order - that there can exist two
; types neither of which is a subtype of the other
(declare-const s Type)
(declare-const t Type)
(assert (and (not (subtype s t)) (not (subtype t s))))

(declare-const root-type Type)

; Original statement from tutorial - declares root-type as a subtype of all types
(push)
(assert (forall ((x Type)) (subtype root-type x)))
(check-sat)
unsat
(pop)
; Corrected statement - declares root-type as the type all other types are subtypes of
(push)
(assert (forall ((x Type)) (subtype x root-type)))
(check-sat)
sat
(get-model)
(model
  ;; universe for Type:
  ;;   Type!val!0 Type!val!3 Type!val!2 Type!val!4 Type!val!1
  ;; -----------
  ;; definitions for universe elements:
  (declare-fun Type!val!0 () Type)
  (declare-fun Type!val!3 () Type)
  (declare-fun Type!val!2 () Type)
  (declare-fun Type!val!4 () Type)
  (declare-fun Type!val!1 () Type)
  ;; cardinality constraint:
  (forall ((x Type))
          (or (= x Type!val!0)
              (= x Type!val!3)
              (= x Type!val!2)
              (= x Type!val!4)
              (= x Type!val!1)))
  ;; -----------
  (define-fun s () Type
    Type!val!0)
  (define-fun t () Type
    Type!val!1)
  (define-fun root-type () Type
    Type!val!2)
  (define-fun subtype!99 ((x!1 Type) (x!2 Type)) Bool
    (ite (and (= x!1 Type!val!1) (= x!2 Type!val!1)) true
    (ite (and (= x!1 Type!val!1) (= x!2 Type!val!2)) true
    (ite (and (= x!1 Type!val!2) (= x!2 Type!val!2)) true
    (ite (and (= x!1 Type!val!0) (= x!2 Type!val!0)) true
    (ite (and (= x!1 Type!val!3) (= x!2 Type!val!3)) true
    (ite (and (= x!1 Type!val!4) (= x!2 Type!val!4)) true
    (ite (and (= x!1 Type!val!3) (= x!2 Type!val!4)) true
    (ite (and (= x!1 Type!val!0) (= x!2 Type!val!2)) true
    (ite (and (= x!1 Type!val!4) (= x!2 Type!val!2)) true
    (ite (and (= x!1 Type!val!3) (= x!2 Type!val!2)) true
      false)))))))))))
  (define-fun k!98 ((x!1 Type)) Type
    (ite (= x!1 Type!val!1) Type!val!1
    (ite (= x!1 Type!val!2) Type!val!2
    (ite (= x!1 Type!val!3) Type!val!3
    (ite (= x!1 Type!val!4) Type!val!4
      Type!val!0)))))
  (define-fun subtype ((x!1 Type) (x!2 Type)) Bool
    (subtype!99 (k!98 x!1) (k!98 x!2)))
  (define-fun array-of!100 ((x!1 Type)) Type
    (ite (= x!1 Type!val!2) Type!val!4
      Type!val!3))
  (define-fun array-of ((x!1 Type)) Type
    (array-of!100 (k!98 x!1)))
)
```

issue_title: How  can I set the different learning rate of each layer in the network?
add support for generic numeric dtype
issue_body:
### Problem/Use Case
There is no way to specify numeric data types as either floating point or integer types i.e. users must specify one or the other. In 1.x, this was accomplished with `dtype: number`, but this has since been dropped.

HDF5IO needs to be updated to except `number`, and more generally, how to handle generic dtypes.  This should start somewhere around here:

https://github.com/NeurodataWithoutBorders/pynwb/blob/f91e0693259f9fef5e13697f15d3b2507385acfb/src/pynwb/form/backends/hdf5/h5tools.py#L404

This is needed for https://github.com/NeurodataWithoutBorders/nwb-schema/issues/178

## Checklist

- [ ] Have you ensured the feature or change was not already [reported](https://github.com/NeurodataWithoutBorders/pynwb/issues/) ?
- [ ] Have you included a brief and descriptive title?
- [ ] Have you included a clear description of the problem you are trying to solve?

issue_title: The best way to override skip_row
Preview Page Mapping Column Values
issue_body:
Hi there, 

Currently, I have a CSV file generated from my crawler with the following fields: 
```
OURPRICE | SALESPRICE | IMG_SRC | BATCH | ASIN | META | URL | NAME | REGULAR_PRICE | DESCRIPTION | SHORT_DESCRIPTION | IDENTIFIER
```

Each row in this file will be mapped to an instance of my "Product" model with the following django fields:
```
class Product(models.Model):
    name = models.TextField()
    regular_price = models.FloatField()
    description = HTMLField(blank=True, null=True)
    short_description = HTMLField(blank=True, null=True)
    batch = models.CharField(max_length=30, blank=True, null=True)
    identifier = models.CharField(max_length=50, blank=True, null=True)
    url = models.TextField(blank=True, null=True)
```

Right now i have the following resource class: 
```
class AmazonProductResource(resources.ModelResource):
    ourprice = Field(column_name='ourprice')
    saleprice = Field(column_name='salesprice')
    img_srcs = Field(column_name='img_src')
    batch = Field(column_name='batch')
    asin = Field(column_name='asin')
    meta = Field(column_name='meta')
    url = Field(column_name='url')

    class Meta:
        model = Product
        exclude = ('id',)
        instance_loader_class = AmazonIdentifierInstanceLoaderClass

    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        """Handling images:
        1. We clear `self._images` at the very beginning of the import process.
        2. We persist images into db at the very end to ensure the Product instance is saved.

        Dry_run:
        1. The first time that this is executed dry_run = True
        2. After confirmation dry_run = False
        """
        self._images = None
        super(*args, **kwargs)

    def before_import_row(self, row, **kwargs):
        self._images = []
        super().before_import_row(row, **kwargs)

    def import_obj(self, obj, data, dry_run):
        """Override this method to map columns to our instance.
        """
        # This must be executed BEFORE assigning images to this obj!
        super().import_obj(obj, data, dry_run)

        price_str = data['ourprice'] or data['saleprice']
        try:
            obj.regular_price = float(price_str.strip('$'))
        except ValueError:
            obj.regular_price = float()

        obj.batch = data['batch']

        obj.identifier = get_amazon_identifier(data['asin'])

        obj.url = data['url']

        try:
            urls = json.loads(data['img_srcs'])
            for i, url in enumerate(urls):
                # verifiy that images don't already exist (during update)
                if not Image.objects.filter(src=url, position=i, product=obj).count():
                    image = Image()
                    image.src = url
                    image.position = i
                    image.product = obj
                    self._images.append(image)
        except json.decoder.JSONDecodeError:
            pass

    def after_save_instance(self, instance, using_transactions, dry_run):
        if not dry_run:
            for image in self._images:
                image.save()
        super().after_save_instance(instance, using_transactions, dry_run)
```

**As you can see, my model doesn't store all the values from my import file.** 

Right now, I am seeing this during import preview: 
![screen shot 2018-04-15 at 5 08 15 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/28204629/38776826-efae9bb2-40cf-11e8-8c84-0ca497972997.png)

This makes it very difficult to see whether the import file has the right values being imported. 

How can I fix this?

issue_title: Reduce package size of @nteract/transform-plotly
issue_body:
I experimented with `plotly` inside Hydrogen and got it working quite well (The few minor things which do not work, are probably blocked by Atom's content security policy. Even with using https://github.com/atom/loophole).

### Problem

`@nteract/transform-plotly` works really great but has a package size of **99MB**! Though we only require `plotly.js/dist/plotly` (a few MB).

For nteract the package size problem will probably be solved by bundling all node modules with `webpack`. But for Hydrogen AFAIK this isn't easily achievable.

### Possible solution

Create a `@nteract/plotly` package which only includes the relevant `dist` file and use this package in `@nteract/transform-plotly`.
We can easily keep the package up to date using a few Travis scripts and greenkeeper.
As a bonus this will heavily reduce the dev install size of nteract and result in a faster install.

What do you think?

issue_title: Nondestructive Editing
issue_body:
## Undo and Snapshots
It would be great if there was an undo history for non-destructive text editing. Currently, the Ctrl-Z shortcut is working but:

* Undo and Redo commands are missing from the **Edit** menu.
* When loading a file consider taking a snapshot of the file as a temp file and add _Revert changes_ to the edit menu.
* Enable the user to save multiple snapshots.

## Are you sure you want to ~~Save file~~ Quit?
When quitting the application the user is asked whether to quit without saving. For people used to Office applications, these options would reduce mistakes at this prompt:
* [Save] [Cancel] [Exit without saving]

## Advanced Options
* A diff tool comparing current file with the snapshot would be an immense help.
* Consider adding an option to push the current file to a remote git repo.

| Firmware Version | 3.21.0.0  | 
| Operating System & Version | Win10 Pro version 1709 build 16299.19  |
| SDK Version | 2.9.0 |

Using VS2017, C++. and COM DLLs, we have implemented the library. We are experiencing random execution exception crashes of 2 kinds (1) at the Windows message loop and (2) at a unknown code location (there is no debug info, even though all projects have PDB files and debug info).  This is a legacy robot program that used to use GigE cameras, so we know our code is very stable. These exceptions make no sense especially at the Windows message loop. How can the message loop be running fine, then suddenly, the message loop doesn't have execution access? Could something be changing the page tables? We have no code that does that. Only standard C++ and WinAPI.

| Build System | CMake 2.8+ | 

Cameras are running at 30FPS (which is what I need).

I get the **Floating point exception (core dumped)** whenever I run the callbacks examples with multiple cameras. I modified the examples so that instead of just one "device," I have a vector of devices, just like in the cpp-multicam example. 
I have a callback per sensor stream (I have three sensors, so I have 9 callback functions).
Everything works (sort of, since sometimes I get the VDBUF error; thats when the program says it "cannot find any devices"...looses USB connection...so when I run it the second/third time, then it can detect all cameras) when I run on color and infrared (6 streams total). But if I stream more than one depth stream (meaning, only one sensor can stream color, IR, and depth, and the other one -- or two-- just color and IR), then I get that error.
I am using a powered USB 3.0 hub.
When I run the cpp-multicam example (cameras running in series), then this does not happen. But if I want to make a video per stream (9 video files total) with OpenCV (save frames to an .avi file), then my FPS drops from 30FPS, to 20FPS. 

What does that error mean? I added exceptions everywhere (both OpenCV and std, etc.), but can't catch anything :-(

issue_title: JWKSet doesn't allow for other keys
issue_body: The RFC explicitly allows the use of other key/value pairs in the JWKSet object. However, the library doesn't allow this. JWKSet should probably just implement the dict interface instead of the set interface.

As show in [this travis test](https://travis-ci.org/vatlab/SoS/jobs/287925981), the `test_task.py TestTask.testKillAndPurge` test failed because `sos` command hang after the jobs are submitted. I cannot reproduce this problem on mac, on linux, even on a [TravisCI virtual machine](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/21053657/how-to-run-travis-ci-locally). I am disabling this test for now but I will keep this ticket open and check it when we touch this part of code later.

issue_title: Change the way fast.ai is documented
issue_body:
Hey all! I recently had a look into documenting python libraries for a work related project. I combined that with digging a little deeper into the fast.ai library. I know that there's already some work done for the documentation and I don't mean to disrespect that, but I thought I share this as an alternative. [Here](http://wp1073433.server-he.de/index.html) you can find a build of the skeleton, which includes all the content from the current /docs directory + [my documentation of the transforms module](http://wp1073433.server-he.de/transforms.html). You can find the source in the /docs folder of [my fastai-fork](https://github.com/KaiLicht/fastai/tree/master/docs).

I'm using sphinx with some extensions. Here's why I think it's a good approach to do it like that:

* **It's easy to get involved:** You don't have to care a lot about the documentation framework. It's almost entirely written in markdown (with some reStructuredText for the autogenerated docstrings). There's one markdown file per module in the [source folder](https://github.com/KaiLicht/fastai/tree/master/docs/source).

* **It's possible and very easy to incorporate autogenerated docstrings.** Sphinx allows to use reStructuredText code blocks to incorporate docstrings. You can individually paste those blocks wherever you feel it's neccessary in the documentation. ([See](https://github.com/KaiLicht/fastai/blob/master/docs/source/transforms.md) the source for the fastai.transforms module)

* **You can include ipython notebooks.** You just have to put the name in the [index.rst](https://github.com/KaiLicht/fastai/blob/master/docs/source/index.rst) file. [As you can see](http://wp1073433.server-he.de/notebooks/TestNotebook.html) that's a pretty neat feature. So even if people do not dive deep into the library they can contribute examples and best practices to show how the library works. 

* **Flexible and easy building and deployment.** You can build the static html files from your fast.ai environment or with a [docker image](https://hub.docker.com/r/kailicht/fastai/~/dockerfile/) I created for this purpose (only one command: `docker run -it --rm -v ~/PathTo/local/fastai:/fastai kailicht/fastai:docs`). It's static html so can be deployed on github.pages, readthedocs (autodeployment) or just on the fast.ai webspace.


I really think that a good documentation is absolutely crucial, especially for projects like fast.ai. It's not only complex in terms of software engineering but also very scientific. Maybe this can be a good starting point for a documentation where people can easily get involved. It's a working skeleton with a framework that gives a lot of room to customize and improve.

@jph00 Let me know if you want me to make a pull request

issue_title: Read retries cause multiple DELETE requests
issue_labels: [{'id': 429586299, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/area:%20code', 'name': 'area: code', 'color': '207de5', 'default': False}, {'id': 429588767, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/resolution:%20fixed', 'name': 'resolution: fixed', 'color': 'cccccc', 'default': False}, {'id': 429587708, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/zhmcclient/python-zhmcclient/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': '84b6eb', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
### Actual behavior

The default values for the retry / timeout configuration specify `read_timeout=300` (sec) and `read_retries=3`.

This configuration causes the "requests" module to resend DELETE requests if the processing takes longer than the `read_timeout`.

This happens for example for the `Partition.delete()` method for a partition with many NICs that had been started and stopped.

### Expected behavior

A DELETE request should not automatically be re-requested after it is already being processed.

A short term mitigation is to change the default configuration to `read_retries=0`, `read_timeout={some large value}`.

A better solution needs to be discussed.

### Execution environment

* zhmcclient version: 0.12.0.dev3
* Operating system (type+version): RHEL 7.2
* HMC version: 2.13.1
* CPC version: 2.13.1

issue_title: social_core.backends.facebook.FacebookOAuth2 should have REDIRECT_STATE set to False
issue_body:
I'm new to Facebook Oauth and actually new to the entire Oauth world, so this bug took me very long time to figure out.

Since I'm new I'm forced to use Facebook API v2.10.

When `REDIRECT_STATE` set to `True`, PSA will compose authentication url like following:

`https://www.facebook.com/v2.10/dialog/oauth?scope=email&redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Fexample.com%3A8000%2Fcomplete%2Ffacebook%2F%3Fredirect_state%3D1TIlS0FPkTctrPCQhgf2rdO3bawWvwdB&client_id=[client_id]&return_scopes=true&state=1TIlS0FPkTctrPCQhgf2rdO3bawWvwdB
`

And Facebook will reject such kind of url, gives error message like following:

> Can't Load URL: The domain of this URL isn't included in the app's domains. To be able to load this URL, add all domains and subdomains of your app to the App Domains field in your app settings.

This the callback url configured on redirect URL doesn't allow `redirect_state` inside the URL, renders the authentication url invalid.

I'm not sure if older version of Facebook API allows this, but this is the case I'm encountering, and when I override the setting `REDIRECT_STATE` with a custom backend, Facebook allows me in.

Following is the entire code I'm using now with Facebook API v2.10:

```python
from social_core.backends.facebook import FacebookOAuth2

class FacebookOAuth2Override(FacebookOAuth2):
    REDIRECT_STATE = False
```

And settings:
```python
SOCIAL_AUTH_FACEBOOK_KEY = 'key'
SOCIAL_AUTH_FACEBOOK_SECRET = 'secret'
SOCIAL_AUTH_FACEBOOK_SCOPE = ['email', 'public_profile']
SOCIAL_AUTH_FACEBOOK_PROFILE_EXTRA_PARAMS = {
  'locale': 'en',
  'fields': 'id,name'
}
```

Please verify and fix, cheers.

issue_title: HLS Playlist doesn't reload often enough if there's a single segment in the playlist.
issue_body:
### Checklist

- [x] This is a bug report.
- [ ] This is a feature request.
- [ ] This is a plugin (improvement) request.
- [ ] I have read the contribution guidelines.

### Description
If an HLS playlist doesn't have more than one 'chunk', then there is a guaranteed underrun because the next reload of the playlist will occur at 'target duration' (https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/blob/2f994acbe5bac2642c0267638fbdfdb7a48a275a/src/streamlink/stream/hls.py#L215)

Which means we will have already run out of content.

### Expected / Actual behavior
Actual: Small stream underrun since we run out of data before we reload the playlist.

Expected: reload the playlist at 1/3 of the 'target duration' if we're playing the last segement in the playlist.


### Reproduction steps / Explicit stream URLs to test
Streams are private, unable to share. if required, i can probably mimic.

In the logs below, the target duration is 11 seconds
```
#EXT-X-TARGETDURATION:11
```
* at 21:37:37, we get the chunks, load a single 'segment'. Download finishes at 21:37:38,990.
* at 21:37:48, we reload the playlist, however, i've already run out of content to play at this point.

### Logs
Sanitized Logs
```
2018-04-21 21:37:37,303 urllib3.connectionpool: DEBUG https://XXX:443 "GET /YYY/chunks.m3u8?wmsAuthSign=ZZZZ HTTP/1.1" 200 1263
2018-04-21 21:37:37,376 streamlink  : DEBUG [stream.hls][debug] Adding segment 8 to queue
2018-04-21 21:37:37,432 urllib3.connectionpool: DEBUG https://XXX "GET /YYY/l_7267045_25681936_8.ts?&wmsAuthSign=ZZZ HTTP/1.1" 200 2475960
2018-04-21 21:37:38,990 streamlink  : DEBUG [stream.hls][debug] Download of segment 8 complete
2018-04-21 21:37:48,377 streamlink  : DEBUG [stream.hls][debug] Reloading playlist
2018-04-21 21:37:48,422 urllib3.connectionpool: DEBUG https://XXX "GET /YYY/chunks.m3u8?wmsAuthSign=ZZZ HTTP/1.1" 200 1263
2018-04-21 21:37:48,462 streamlink  : DEBUG [stream.hls][debug] Adding segment 9 to queue
2018-04-21 21:37:48,507 urllib3.connectionpool: DEBUG https://XXX "GET /YYY/l_7267045_25691946_9.ts?wmsAuthSign=ZZZ HTTP/1.1" 200 2547776
2018-04-21 21:37:50,570 streamlink  : DEBUG [stream.hls][debug] Download of segment 9 complete
```

### Comments, screenshots, etc.
A super quick and dirty patch at line 219:
```
        #if not self.playlist_changed:
        self.playlist_reload_time = max(self.playlist_reload_time / 3, 1)
```
Seems to work to always reload the playlist at 1/3 of the target duration, but this isn't the most efficient if there's enough data in the playlist.

[Love Streamlink? Please consider supporting our collective. Thanks!](https://opencollective.com/streamlink/donate)

issue_title: About LPversion and LPhelp
issue_body:
`from LightPipes import *` isn't good practice in python, although it seems nicer in this project, I prefer
`import LightPipes as lp` because it follows the convention of most python libraries.

The biggest problem of `import *` is it will pollute global namespace, we can add [\_\_all\_\_](https://docs.python.org/2/tutorial/modules.html#importing-from-a-package) to
`__init__.py`  to avoid this(LPversion and LPhelp look ugly).

Do you prefer convenience or comprehensive?

issue_title: 7.0.0: Cannot load notebook with saved widget output
issue_body:
Hello,

We have a problem with the new version of ipywidgets (or more likely with the widgetsnbextension), where a notebook with a saved widget output cannot be loaded.

When loading such a notebook the following error message is thrown in the browser (Firefox):

```
Notebook failed to load

The error was:
TypeError: Jupyter.notebook.kernel is null

See the error console for details.
```

It occurs in widgetsnbextension's [render](https://github.com/jupyter-widgets/ipywidgets/blob/master/widgetsnbextension/src/extension.js#L105) function. It seems as if the kernel is not yet ready when the widget manager property is accessed.

We have the following library versions for Python2 and Python3 respectively:
```
root@de79f18a1f6a:~# pip2 show ipython notebook nbformat ipywidgets widgetsnbextension traitlets | grep Version -B1

```

We use Python2 kernels for our notebooks, and here is an example notebook with which we can reproduce the problem: https://gist.github.com/ak0ska/efd644222cbbe936c822f941c3e708fa

If I clear all output, save and reload the notebook, there is no error message. If I execute all cells, save the notebook and reload, I get the error message. The example works without problems with ipywdgets 6 and widgetsnbextension 2.

Any ideas what might be wrong?

Can't access DynamoDB Local Shell
issue_body:
The localstack [change log](https://github.com/localstack/localstack#change-log) shows that localstack uses [DynamoDB Local](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/DynamoDBLocal.html) under the hood as of v0.5.0

> ... replace dynalite with DynamoDBLocal ...

DynamoDB Local is super useful, and has a [built in web GUI](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/sweet-treats-for-dynamodb-users/) accessible at http://localhost:8000 by default. This works great when using DynamoDB Local _standalone_.

```
# Launch DynamoDB Local _without_ localstack
java -Djava.library.path=./DynamoDBLocal_lib -jar DynamoDBLocal.jar -sharedDb
# navigate to http://localhost:8000/shell
```

Since localstack uses DynamoDB Local under the hood. I was hoping that same web UI shell would be accessible when running Dynamo via localstack. However, I'm having issues with that.

```
docker run -p 4569:4569 localstack/localstack:latest
```

When I try to hit http://localhost:4569/shell in a browser the connection times out.

I see [other users can use the DynamoDB shell in localstack](https://github.com/localstack/localstack/issues/294) so clearly it _can_ work, but when I try running using the latest image, I just get an error every time I hit http://localhost:4569/shell

```
2018-03-21T17:43:31:ERROR:localstack.services.generic_proxy: Error forwarding request: expected string or buffer Traceback (most recent call last):

    return _default_decoder.decode(s)

    obj, end = self.raw_decode(s, idx=_w(s, 0).end())
TypeError: expected string or buffer
```

I tried [explicitly running kinesis with dynamo](https://github.com/localstack/localstack/issues/294), but that made no difference. I encountered the same issues. Same thing if I tried running DynamoDB on a different port using `SERVICES=dynamodb:8000`

I tried some of the other [suggested ways of starting up localstack](https://github.com/localstack/localstack#installing) to no avail.

```
# Using the localstack binary to spin up the docker images
pip install localstack
TMPDIR=/private$TMPDIR localstack start --docker

# Running the infrastructure from the localstack binary
pip install localstack
localstack start
```

Regardless of how I start localstack I see hints that DynamoDB Local is running on 4569, but I can't hit the shell.

```
Starting mock DynamoDB (http port 4569)...
```

Am I just missing something? Am I using the wrong port?

issue_title: Remove circle proto message dependency due to "Graph" attribute type
issue_body:
In ONNX, we're having a different bunch of types for attributes from input/output types. This introduces two main problems.
1. Circle dependency among proto messages. all messages in onnx.proto have to be put in one file due to this.
2. Attribute can't have flexible type per input/output type. For example, “max” and "min" attribute in Clip op. It should have same type as input/output.

Solution:  Remove AttributeProto, having TensorProto for attributes too. In order to not make a big change like this, the first step may be, Remove "Graph" attribute type. All places referring a graph attribute will use "String" (graph name) to refer to a graph, and the graph referred will be located in a model separately.

Thoughts?

issue_title: RNN layers don't work when output units != hidden state units
issue_body:
According to [the documentation of tf.contrib.rnn.RNNCell](https://www.tensorflow.org/api_docs/python/tf/contrib/rnn/RNNCell), there is no guarantee that an RNN cell has the same number of output units as hidden state units.

I know that in common use cases they are almost always equal, but when I write a custom RNN cell which outputs its internal state alongside the ordinary output (and thus has more output units than hidden state units), all RNN layer APIs fail to work because they do many incorrect reshaping.

For example (part of [DynamicRNNLayer](https://github.com/zsdonghao/tensorlayer/blob/master/tensorlayer/layers.py#L4003)):
```python
          with tf.variable_scope(name, initializer=initializer) as vs:
            outputs, last_states = tf.nn.dynamic_rnn(
                cell=self.cell,
                # inputs=X
                inputs = self.inputs,
                # dtype=tf.float64,
                sequence_length=sequence_length,
                initial_state = self.initial_state,
                **dynamic_rnn_init_args
                )
            rnn_variables = tf.get_collection(TF_GRAPHKEYS_VARIABLES, scope=vs.name)

            # print("     n_params : %d" % (len(rnn_variables)))
            # Manage the outputs
            if return_last:
                # [batch_size, n_hidden]
                # outputs = tf.transpose(tf.pack(outputs), [1, 0, 2]) # TF1.0 tf.pack --> tf.stack
                self.outputs = advanced_indexing_op(outputs, sequence_length)
            else:
                # [batch_size, n_step(max), n_hidden]
                # self.outputs = result[0]["outputs"]
                # self.outputs = outputs    # it is 3d, but it is a list
                if return_seq_2d:
                    # PTB tutorial:
                    # 2D Tensor [n_example, n_hidden]
                    try: # TF1.0
                        self.outputs = tf.reshape(tf.concat(outputs, 1), [-1, n_hidden])
                    except: # TF0.12
                        self.outputs = tf.reshape(tf.concat(1, outputs), [-1, n_hidden])
                else:
                    # <akara>:
                    # 3D Tensor [batch_size, n_steps(max), n_hidden]
                    max_length = tf.shape(outputs)[1]
                    batch_size = tf.shape(outputs)[0]


                    try: # TF1.0
                        self.outputs = tf.reshape(tf.concat(outputs, 1), [batch_size, max_length, n_hidden])
                    except: # TF0.12
                        self.outputs = tf.reshape(tf.concat(1, outputs), [batch_size, max_length, n_hidden])
```
The Tensorflow API `tf.nn.dynamic_rnn` actually handles dimensions correctly, but these `n_hidden`s in reshaping messed it up. They should be replaced by something like `tf.shape(outputs)[-1]`.

This seems to be a low priority bug, but I feel like that I am urged to fix it. Do you think it is necessary? @zsdonghao

issue_title: removal of NR gaussjinv from meas_shapes_hsm psfcorr.c
issue_body:
Currently the meas_shapes_hsm code relies on one NR routine, gaussjinv.  (There are several NR codes in there that are officially open-source, such as dvector, ivector, dmatrix, etc. - those don't have to be removed, though we may wish to later on.)  We should make the code use TMV for matrix inversion instead of gaussjinv; the result is that the code will probably be faster, anyway.   

Please note: the ONLY place that the code requires gaussjinv is for the shapelets PSF correction, which is only one of several possible methods.  If there is no clear and simple way to do this using TMV, we should simply remove gaussjinv and live without a shapelets PSF correction (the implementation in this code was not, in any case, very well tested, since we never used it for science!).  This would be preferable in my opinion to someone spending a lot of time on getting this to work with TMV.  Opinions on just how hard this replacement of gaussjinv --> TMV would be are therefore valuable!

If we choose to replace gaussjinv with something from TMV, this is probably a job for Mike.  If we choose to rip it out and not have PSF correction via shapelets from this code, then this is a job for Rachel.

issue_title: Can't define declarative schema with non python identifiers as name
issue_body:
Here's an example

```python
        class FnordSchema(colander.Schema):
            fnord = colander.SchemaNode(
                colander.Sequence(), 
                colander.SchemaNode(colander.Integer(), name='integer in sequence'), 
                name="fnord[]"))
```

Instead I have to use this workaround:
 
```python
        class FnordSchema(colander.Schema):
        schema = FnordSchema()
        schema.add(colander.SchemaNode(
                colander.Sequence(), 
                colander.SchemaNode(colander.Integer(), name='integer in sequence'), 
                name="fnord[]"))
```

Ideally the parsed value would be available under the name 'fnord' instead of 'fnord[]', but that would be an exciter. :-)
